architecture_decisions.decision: Microservices architecture with Go (Golang) for backend services
architecture_decisions.rationale: Go provides excellent concurrency for handling multiple print jobs, small binary size for agent deployment, strong HTTP/2 and WebSocket support, and is the language of choice for cloud-native services (Docker, Kubernetes).

architecture_decisions.decision: PostgreSQL as primary database with Redis for caching and queues
architecture_decisions.rationale: PostgreSQL provides robust JSONB support for printer capabilities and job metadata, excellent reliability for transactional operations, and native UUID support. Redis enables fast job queuing and real-time status tracking.

architecture_decisions.decision: WebSocket-based real-time communication using Centrifugo or custom WebSocket server
architecture_decisions.rationale: Print jobs require real-time status updates from agent to cloud to client. WebSocket provides bidirectional low-latency communication essential for print job progress tracking.
Cloud service routes encrypted print jobs without ability to decrypt. Client encrypts with printer's public key, only agent with private key can decrypt. Addresses privacy-first market positioning.
All clients convert to PDF before upload. Agent converts PDF to printer-specific format. Eliminates driver compatibility issues on client side, reduces bandwidth with compression.
High-performance binary protocol with built-in code generation. Reduces latency between microservices compared to REST+JSON.

architecture_decisions.decision: S3-compatible object storage (MinIO for self-hosted, AWS S3/Azure Blob for cloud)
architecture_decisions.rationale: Print job data storage needs scalable object storage with lifecycle policies for auto-deletion. S3 API is industry standard enabling multi-cloud deployment.

architecture_decisions.decision: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite for web dashboard
architecture_decisions.rationale: Modern stack with fast development experience, excellent type safety for complex admin interfaces, and smaller bundle sizes for better performance.

architecture_decisions.decision: React Native with Expo for iOS/Android mobile apps
architecture_decisions.rationale: Single codebase for both platforms, Expo simplifies build and deployment. Addresses market gap for mobile printing.

backend_tasks.purpose: Root Go module definition with all service dependencies
Shared error types and handling across services
OpenTelemetry tracing middleware for all services
OIDC/OAuth2 integration for Azure AD, Google Workspace
Print job queue and routing service
Background job processor for routing jobs to agents
Storage service for print job data
Windows print agent main entry point
Encrypted print job upload to cloud
OpenAPI 3.1 specification for all REST APIs
gRPC service definitions for printer registry
gRPC service definitions for job queue

frontend_tasks.purpose: Print job history and status page
WebSocket connection hook for real-time updates

database_migrations.sql:
CREATE TABLE organizations (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  slug VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  plan VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'free' CHECK (plan IN ('free', 'pro', 'enterprise')),
  settings JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  sso_config JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  max_users INTEGER DEFAULT 10,
  max_printers INTEGER DEFAULT 5,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_organizations_slug ON organizations(slug);

CREATE TABLE users (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  password_hash VARCHAR(255),
  name VARCHAR(255),
  role VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'user' CHECK (role IN ('user', 'admin', 'owner')),
  org_id UUID REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  email_verified BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  sso_provider VARCHAR(50),
  sso_id VARCHAR(255),
  page_quota_monthly INTEGER DEFAULT 1000,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_org ON users(org_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_sso ON users(sso_provider, sso_id);

CREATE TABLE agents (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'offline' CHECK (status IN ('online', 'offline', 'error')),
  platform VARCHAR(50),
  platform_version VARCHAR(50),
  agent_version VARCHAR(50),
  ip_address INET,
  last_heartbeat TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  capabilities JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  public_key TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_agents_org ON agents(org_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_agents_status ON agents(status);

CREATE TABLE printers (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  agent_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  type VARCHAR(50) CHECK (type IN ('usb', 'network', 'virtual')),
  driver VARCHAR(255),
  port VARCHAR(100),
  capabilities JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  is_online BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  last_seen TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(agent_id, name)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_printers_org ON printers(org_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_printers_agent ON printers(agent_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_printers_active ON printers(is_active, is_online);

CREATE TABLE print_jobs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  printer_id UUID REFERENCES printers(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'queued' CHECK (status IN ('queued', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled')),
  document_name VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  document_type VARCHAR(50),
  page_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  color_pages INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  file_size BIGINT DEFAULT 0,
  file_hash VARCHAR(64),
  storage_key VARCHAR(500),
  encryption_key TEXT,
  settings JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  error_message TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  auto_delete_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE
);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_user ON print_jobs(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_printer ON print_jobs(printer_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_org ON print_jobs(org_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_status ON print_jobs(status, created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_auto_delete ON print_jobs(auto_delete_at) WHERE auto_delete_at IS NOT NULL;

CREATE TABLE usage_stats (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  printer_id UUID REFERENCES printers(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  stat_date DATE NOT NULL,
  pages_printed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  color_pages INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  jobs_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  jobs_completed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  jobs_failed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  total_bytes BIGINT DEFAULT 0,
  estimated_cost DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
  co2_grams DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
  trees_saved DECIMAL(10,4) DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(org_id, user_id, printer_id, stat_date)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_usage_stats_date ON usage_stats(stat_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_usage_stats_org ON usage_stats(org_id, stat_date);

CREATE TABLE api_keys (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  key_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  scopes TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  last_used_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_api_keys_user ON api_keys(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_api_keys_org ON api_keys(org_id);

CREATE TABLE webhooks (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  url VARCHAR(2048) NOT NULL,
  secret VARCHAR(255),
  events TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  last_triggered_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  failure_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_webhooks_org ON webhooks(org_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_webhooks_active ON webhooks(org_id, is_active);

CREATE TABLE devices (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  device_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (device_type IN ('ios', 'android', 'web')),
  device_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  push_token TEXT,
  push_provider VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'fcm' CHECK (push_provider IN ('fcm', 'apns', 'none')),
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  last_used_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(user_id, device_id)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_devices_user ON devices(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_devices_push ON devices(push_token) WHERE push_token IS NOT NULL;

cases: HashPassword generates different hashes for same password
VerifyPassword returns true for correct password
VerifyPassword returns false for incorrect password
Create inserts user with hashed password
GetByEmail returns error when not found
ProcessPendingJobs routes jobs to online agents
Different keypairs cannot decrypt each other

integration_tests.name: Printer permission flow
steps: User submits job to permitted printer

e2e_tests.name: Multi-user organization printing
steps: Admin grants printer access to users

e2e_tests.name: Webhook delivery
steps: Verify webhook called with correct payload

security_notes: All API endpoints require authentication except /auth/*
Rate limiting: 100 requests per minute per user for uploads, 1000 per minute for reads
Print job data encrypted client-side before upload using recipient printer's public key (NaCl Box)
TLS 1.3 required for all connections, minimum cipher suite TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
Passwords hashed with argon2id (time=3, memory=64MB, parallelism=4)
JWT access tokens expire after 15 minutes, refresh tokens expire after 30 days
Refresh tokens stored with hash, not plaintext
Audit logs immutable, retained 90 days per SOC2 requirements
Print jobs auto-delete 5 minutes after completion, storage key expires via S3 lifecycle
Webhook deliveries use HMAC signatures for payload verification
API keys stored with SHA-256 hash, prefix shown for identification
Session IP addresses tracked for anomaly detection
SSO configurations validated before use, only HTTPS callbacks allowed
File upload limit: 100MB per job, validated before encryption
Input validation on all fields, SQL injection prevented via parameterized queries
CORS restricted to configured origins per organization
CSP headers set on all responses, strict mode for production
docker_changes: Dockerfile for each microservice (auth-service, registry-service, job-service, storage-service, notification-service, organization-service, analytics-service, gateway)
docker-compose.yml for local development with PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO
Multi-stage builds for Go services to minimize image size
Alpine-based images for Windows/Linux agent binaries
Secrets management via environment variables or Docker secrets
Volume mounts for persistent data (PostgreSQL data, Redis persistence, MinIO storage)
Network isolation: services on internal network, only gateway exposed
market_driven_features: End-to-end encryption (TLS 1.3+ + NaCl) - critical priority competitor parity
SOC2/ISO27001 compliance foundation - critical priority enterprise requirement
SAML 2.0 and OIDC SSO integration - high priority Azure AD/Google Workspace support
Cross-platform agents (Windows, macOS, Linux) - high priority feature gap
REST API with webhooks - high priority developer/automation requirement
Environmental impact reporting - medium priority ESG appeal (trees_saved, co2_grams)
Self-hosted deployment option - high priority unique selling point vs SaaS competitors
Mobile apps (React Native with Expo) - high priority BYOD workflow support
Multi-tenant architecture with RBAC - high priority enterprise requirement
Secure print release (PIN-based) - medium priority shared environment privacy
Print quotas and cost allocation - medium priority admin control (PaperCut parity)
Offline queue caching - medium priority unreliable network handling
